decks.notes: This deck focuses on using disruptive spells like Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Counterspells to control the game while cycling through its library with cards like Snapcaster Mage and Stock Up. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional value and recursion from the graveyard. The deck also includes various forms of removal like Fatal Push and Feed the Swarm to deal with threats, as well as card advantage engines like Psychic Frog and Ancestral Recall to maintain resources and outvalue the opponent.

This deck focuses on using cards like Paradoxical Outcome and Underworld Breach to generate a massive amount of mana and draw cards in order to assemble a game-winning combo. It also includes disruption spells like Force of Will and Chain of Vapor to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional recursion and value. Overall, the deck seeks to quickly and efficiently assemble its combo pieces to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful control cards and counterspells to disrupt the opponent's strategy while generating card advantage with cards like Snapcaster Mage and Psychic Frog. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion adds flexibility and resilience to the deck. The mana base is optimized for consistency and includes powerful mana rocks like the Moxen. The deck's goal is to control the game through efficient removal and disruptive spells, ultimately outvaluing the opponent and winning through attrition.

This Vintage 1 vs 1 deck focuses on using artifact-based combo pieces like Time Vault, Bolas's Citadel, and The One Ring to generate massive amounts of mana and draw cards with Paradoxical Outcome. The key strategy is to assemble the combo pieces quickly while controlling the opponent's actions with counterspells like Force of Will and Misdirection. Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional utility and win conditions, making this deck a versatile and powerful threat in a fast-paced format.

The deck focuses on generating a large amount of mana using powerful artifacts like Black Lotus, Moxen, and Grim Monolith to ramp into casting Coveted Jewel as quickly as possible. Once Coveted Jewel is in play, the deck aims to capitalize on its ability to draw cards and generate even more mana to fuel powerful combo plays with cards like Paradoxical Outcome and Tinker. The deck also includes control elements like Force of Will and disruptive cards like Sink into Stupor to protect and enable its combo win condition.

This deck revolves around using artifacts and powerful spells to generate mana quickly, disrupt opponents, and ultimately win through a powerful combo involving Coveted Jewel. The deck includes powerful mana accelerants like the Moxen and Mishra's Workshop, along with control elements like Force of Will and Transmute Artifact. The synergy between cards like Paradox Engine, Time Vault, and Coveted Jewel allows for explosive plays that can overwhelm opponents. The deck aims to assemble its combo pieces quickly and efficiently to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent.

The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan and control the battlefield through counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. The deck focuses on utilizing powerful cards like Force of Will, Fatal Push, and Ancestral Recall to outmaneuver the opponent and maintain control of the board. With access to disruptive creatures like Psychic Frog and Orcish Bowmasters, as well as powerful utility lands like Wasteland and Undercity Sewers, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent in a long game.

This deck focuses on assembling the Doomsday combo to win the game quickly by using a combination of card draw, counterspells, and manipulation of the top cards of the library. The key goal is to resolve Doomsday, then use cards like Thassa's Oracle and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to win the game with a strategic stack of cards. The deck also includes powerful cards like Force of Will, Ancestral Recall, and Demonic Tutor to support the combo and protect it from disruption.

The deck focuses on disrupting opponents with countermagic and removal spells, while deploying efficient threats and card advantage engines. It aims to control the game early on with disruption from cards like Force of Will and Fatal Push, then establish a board presence with creatures like Collector Ouphe and Psychic Frog. The deck also utilizes powerful spells like Ancestral Recall and Demonic Tutor to maintain advantage and finish the game quickly with aggressive plays. It aims to outvalue opponents through a combination of disruption, card advantage, and efficient threats.

The deck focuses on assembling powerful artifacts and utilizing them to generate mana and draw cards through Paradoxical Outcome. By combining Coveted Jewel with The One Ring and other artifacts like Time Vault and Grim Monolith, the deck can create a massive advantage, eventually overwhelming the opponent. The inclusion of powerful control elements such as Force of Will and Sink into Stupor ensures that the combo can be protected and executed successfully. Additionally, access to tutors like Tinker and Transmute Artifact provides consistency in finding key pieces of the combo.

The deck focuses on utilizing control elements such as counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to disrupt the opponent's game plan while also establishing a strong board presence. It also utilizes the synergy between Lurrus of the Dream-Den and low-cost permanents to create a recurring advantage. The inclusion of disruptive cards such as Null Rod and Nihil Spellbomb further enhances the control aspect of the deck, allowing it to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent in a 1 vs 1 setting. Ultimately, the goal is to control the game until victory is secured.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through a combination of countermagic, removal spells, and hand disruption. By controlling the board and manipulating the opponent's resources, the deck seeks to establish a dominant position and close out the game with efficient threats like Snapcaster Mage or Orcish Bowmasters. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides additional value and resilience in longer games. With a streamlined mana base and powerful spells, this Dimir Control deck is designed to outmaneuver and outlast opposing decks in a competitive Vintage 1 vs 1 format.

This Doomsday combo deck aims to win quickly by using cards like Thassa's Oracle and Doomsday to set up a winning pile and win the game in a single turn. The key strategy involves using efficient card draw and disruption spells to find and protect the combo pieces while controlling the opponent's board. The deck also utilizes Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion to provide additional value and consistency to the strategy. Overall, the deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo and executing it efficiently to secure victory.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through counterspells and removal while generating card advantage through card draw and recursion. By controlling the board and denying the opponent's resources, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and slowly chip away at their life total. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional value from the graveyard, giving the deck resilience and flexibility in the late game. The deck's key strength lies in its ability to adapt to different matchups and effectively answer threats while maintaining a strong board presence.

This deck focuses on utilizing control elements to slow down the opponent while setting up powerful graveyard interactions with Gifts Ungiven and Underworld Breach. The key strategy involves efficiently managing resources with powerful mana accelerants like the Moxen and Lotus cards, along with efficient spell choices like Force of Will and Mana Drain. The deck aims to outvalue opponents by utilizing versatile cards like Lurrus of the Dream-Den and key combo pieces like Brain Freeze for potential mill win conditions. The deck also has access to powerful tutors to find key cards and disrupt opponents' strategies efficiently.

This deck aims to efficiently generate mana, protect its key pieces with countermagic, and ultimately win the game through explosive card draw and casting big spells. The combination of Paradoxical Outcome and cards like Mana Crypt, Moxen, and artifacts allows for rapid mana generation and card advantage. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect the combo and deal with opposing threats. The goal is to utilize these pieces to assemble a powerful board state and overwhelm the opponent quickly.
